MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2003 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patricia Barlow, Esq., 977-1108, for defendant Tutor-Saliba Corp. moving for summary judgment
James G. Butler, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF WILLIAM L. VEEN 673-4800, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA CORP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT -- previous tentative was adopted, and this motion was continued for hearing re: causation. Court now rules: Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of fact re: causation. Plaintiff is to prepare an order pursuant to CCP 437c(g). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (DAG/301/KG-X) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2003 Continued To Feb-05-03 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patricia Barlow, Esq., 977-1108, for defendant Tutor-Saliba Corporation moving for summary judgment
James G. Butler, Jr., 673-4800, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified -- DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA CORP.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT -- Motion for summary judgment is
denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied, triable issue of fact re: Control
of "Ramp" and location of accident. The Court then continued the hearing re: the issue of causation (whether the "ramp" contributed to the injury) to February 5, 2003. Plaintiff is to submit the declaration by 2/3, Monday, and defendant to submit the deposition
transcript as soon as possible. (DAG/301/KG-X) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780
